phrases.intent_en: Cheer your horse at the start
phrases.text_local: Et ils sont partis ! Allez, allez !
phrases.pronunciation: ay eel son par-TEE! ah-LAY, ah-LAY!
phrases.cultural_note: 'Et ils sont partis !' (And they're off!) is the classic French racing cry. Keeneland's French-influenced thoroughbred tradition makes this especially fitting.

phrases.intent_en: Place a bet at the window
phrases.text_local: Un pari gagnant sur le numéro trois, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: un pah-REE gah-NYON soor luh new-may-ROH TWAH, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: A 'gagnant' bet means you're picking a horse to win outright. At French racecourses (Chantilly, Longchamp), betting windows look much like Keeneland's.

responses.english: Here is your ticket. Good luck!

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the next race is
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure part la prochaine course ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel uhr par lah pro-SHEN koors
phrases.cultural_note: French racing uses 'course' for race, 'partant' for runner. The question structure 'à quelle heure' is one of the most useful time-asking phrases.

responses.french: La prochaine course est dans vingt minutes.
responses.english: The next race is in twenty minutes.
responses.phonetic: lah pro-SHEN koors eh don van mee-NEWT

phrases.intent_en: Admire a horse in the paddock
phrases.text_local: Quel beau cheval ! Regardez comme il est fort.
phrases.pronunciation: kel boh shuh-VAL! ruh-gar-DAY kom eel ay for
phrases.cultural_note: The paddock ('le paddock') is where horses are saddled before a race — same word in French and English. Admiring horses is universal at any racecourse.
'On a gagné' uses 'on' (we/one) — the informal collective pronoun French people use constantly instead of 'nous'.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the odds on a horse
phrases.text_local: Quelle est la cote du numéro sept ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel ay lah kot dew new-may-ROH set
phrases.cultural_note: 'La cote' means the odds in French horse racing. France's premier racing publication is called 'Paris Turf'.

responses.french: Il est à cinq contre un.
responses.phonetic: eel ay ah sank kon-truh UN

phrases.text_local: Un bourbon avec des glaçons, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: un boor-BON ah-vek day glah-SON, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Keeneland is known for its mint juleps, but bourbon is king in Kentucky. 'Des glaçons' means ice cubes — a phrase useful across France too.
